headlines.title: House passes Ukraine aid bill in another GOP rebuke of Trump’s foreign policy
headlines.description: The vote comes after the House passed a resolution earlier this week against President Donald Trump’s war against Iran.

headlines.title: Democrats navigate a new Graham Platner controversy in Maine’s battleground Senate race
headlines.description: For the Democratic Party, the road to Maine’s Senate primary is paved in dread.

headlines.title: Some Republicans cast votes against Trump priorities as Senate takes up ICE and Border Patrol bill
headlines.description: Some Republicans want to amend the bill to prohibit the Trump administration from setting up the “anti-weaponization” fund that could pay Jan. 6 rioters.

headlines.title: Trump says Bill Pulte will serve only in an acting capacity as DNI
headlines.description: The controversial pick drew bipartisan pushback when Trump announced he would nominate him to the position permanently.

headlines.title: Kornacki goes over latest numbers for two key races in California
headlines.description: With California still counting votes for two key races, NBC News' Steve Kornacki shares the latest updates on the numbers and where each candidate stands.

headlines.title: A sneak peek at the new Obama Presidential Center in Chicago
headlines.description: Former Pres. Barack Obama's presidential center is set to open on the South Side of Chicago on Juneteenth, despite some pushback from the community. NBC News' Shaquille Brewster gets an inside look at the museum, library, and campus

headlines.title: John Bolton to plead guilty in classified documents case
headlines.description: Two sources familiar with the matter say former Trump National Security Advisor John Bolton, a fierce Trump critic, will now plead guilty to one count of unlawfully retaining national security information. NBC News' Garrett Haake reports.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: House committee calls on DOJ to investigate two men with ties to Jeffrey Epstein
headlines.description: The committee asked for the probe after Sarah Kellen, Epstein’s former assistant, testified that she’d been preyed upon by three men, one of whom is dead.

headlines.title: Cracks emerge in GOP support for Trump’s agenda: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, Jonathan Allen explores how congressional Republicans are starting to push back on some of President Donald Trump’s wishes.

headlines.title: CIA puts senior officials on leave over officer arrested with gold bars
headlines.description: CIA officer David Rush is scheduled to appear in court Friday after the FBI said it found $40 million in gold bars stashed at his Virginia home.

headlines.title: Kennedy Center lawyers tell staff to remove references to Trump in signage
headlines.description: Attorneys for the iconic performing arts venue said the signage must revert to previous names like The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts by June 12.
